mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is particularly useful for developers looking to create complex AI workflows without extensive coding. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and potential resource consumption based on the complexity of the AI agents being built.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine, allowing you to manage Flowise applications easily.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and configured.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys Flowise to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/Flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Ensure you have a monitoring configuration file ready.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated. Monitor your usage to avoid unexpected charges.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Flowise deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the Flowise release.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process will involve draining the nodes, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the deployment.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl get pods --namespace flowise

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues or incorrect kubeconfig.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure that the cluster is running with 'gcloud container clusters list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: release flowise failed: timed out waiting for the condition'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Flowise deployment is not progressing, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the pods with 'kubectl get pods --namespace flowise' and inspect logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Custom Resource Definitions required for Flowise are not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f crds.yaml' before installing Flowise.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: failed to connect to Flowise service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Flowise service is not exposed correctly, possibly due to incorrect service type.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc --namespace flowise' and ensure it is of type LoadBalancer.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved UI for agent creation.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API endpoints for agent management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your existing agents using the provided migration tool.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods --namespace flowise' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
